page.title: Creator — Build Prescription
page.description: Build each FHIR Resource step by step, then assemble into a Document Bundle
page.successMessage: Document Bundle submitted successfully! Bundle ID:
page.successHint: Go to the Consumer module to query this prescription
page.draftHint: Unfinished work is automatically saved as a local draft.
page.draftSaved: You can continue editing next time.
page.draftRestoredDesc: An unfinished Creator workflow was restored from local storage.
page.draftRestoredDescWithTime: An unfinished Creator workflow was restored from local storage. Last saved: {{time}}
page.resetConfirmDesc: All created Resources will be cleared. This cannot be undone.

stepper.jsonShowingLast: Showing only the latest updated resource: {{resource}}
stepper.jsonShowLatestHint: Show only the latest updated resource: {{resource}}
stepper.jsonShowLatestUnavailable: No latest resource is available yet

organization.introHint: Enter the healthcare organization responsible for this prescription. The organization code should ideally be a stable internal or institutional identifier.
organization.example1: National Taiwan University Hospital / NTUH001 / Hospital

name.label: Institution Name
name.hint: Enter the formal organization name that clinicians and patients can recognize.

identifier.label: Institution Code
identifier.hint: Use a stable code already used by your system or institution.

type.label: Institution Type
type.hint: Choose the organization type that best matches the real setting, such as hospital, clinic, or pharmacy.

patient.introHint: The patient is the subject of the prescription. In this project, Patient.identifier is represented by the student ID.
patient.example1: Family Name: Wang / Given Name: Xiao Ming / Student ID: S1101001
patient.example2: If there is no separate medical record number, the student ID is used as the primary identifier here.

familyName.label: Family Name
familyName.hint: Enter the family name portion of the patient's name.

givenName.label: Given Name
givenName.hint: Enter the given name portion of the patient's name.

studentId.label: Student ID (Identifier)
studentId.hint: ★ Per spec, Patient.identifier uses student ID

gender.label: Gender
gender.hint: Choose the value that best matches the patient's demographic record.

birthDate.label: Birth Date
birthDate.hint: Birth date helps distinguish patients and is also shown in the Consumer detail view.

practitioner.introHint: Enter the prescribing clinician here. The license number is used as the main practitioner identifier.
practitioner.example1: Lee / Da Ren / MED20001 / General Practice
practitioner.example2: Qualification can be a readable specialty name, such as Internal Medicine or Family Medicine.

licenseNumber.label: License Number
licenseNumber.hint: Enter a license or registration number that identifies this practitioner.

qualification.label: Qualification
qualification.hint: Enter the specialty or qualification in human-readable form.

encounter.introHint: Use this step to describe the care setting for the prescription. For a typical clinic visit, choose ambulatory and enter the actual start and end times.
encounter.example2: If only the start time is known, you can enter the start time first and leave the end time empty.

type.label: Visit Type
type.hint: Choose ambulatory, emergency, or inpatient based on the real care setting.

startDate.label: Visit Start Date
startDate.hint: Enter the date and time when the visit or encounter started.

endDate.label: Visit End Date
endDate.hint: Enter the end time when known, or leave it blank if it is not available.

condition.introHint: ICD-10 is the formal diagnosis classification. If you only know the diagnosis name, look up the matching code first and then enter it here.
condition.presetsHint: The presets above are only quick examples. Before submitting, confirm the ICD-10 diagnosis code against the actual chart.

icdCode.label: ICD-10 Code
icdCode.hint: Enter a standard ICD-10 diagnosis code, such as J06.9, I10, or E11.9.

diagnosisName.label: Diagnosis Name
diagnosisName.hint: Enter the diagnosis name that matches the ICD-10 code.

observation.introHint: The LOINC code identifies what was measured, while the value and unit describe the actual result recorded for this visit.
observation.presetsHint: LOINC codes identify the lab or measurement item. Use standard codes whenever possible.

loincCode.label: LOINC Code
loincCode.hint: Enter a standard LOINC code, such as 8867-4 for heart rate or 8480-6 for systolic blood pressure.

itemName.label: Item Name
itemName.hint: The item name should match the selected LOINC code.

value.label: Value
value.hint: Enter the measured result, such as 120 for blood pressure, 72 for heart rate, or 65 for body weight.

unit.label: Unit
unit.hint: Use common clinical units such as mmHg, mg/dL, or kg.

coverage.introHint: Use this step to record the patient's insurance identity. For National Health Insurance, enter the card number or another insurance ID that identifies the coverage record.
coverage.example1: National Health Insurance: choose National Health Insurance (NHI) and enter the NHI card number or coverage ID.
coverage.example2: If there is a validity period, enter the expiry date. If there is no fixed end date, only the effective date is enough.

type.label: Insurance Type
type.hint: Choose the insurance type most relevant to this visit, such as National Health Insurance or self-pay.

insuranceId.label: NHI Card / Insurance ID
insuranceId.hint: Enter an insurance identifier, such as an NHI card number or member ID.

effectiveDate.label: Effective Date
effectiveDate.hint: Use the date when the coverage becomes valid. If it is only relevant to this visit, the visit date is usually acceptable.

expiryDate.label: Expiry Date
expiryDate.hint: Leave this empty when the coverage has no explicit end date.

medication.introHint: This step creates the medication master record. Decide whether you are using ATC or NHI drug codes first, then enter the matching code and name.
medication.presetsHint: The presets above are shortcuts for common examples. Confirm the actual medication and code before submitting.
medication.example2: Amoxicillin can use J01CA04, then choose the real dose form such as capsule or suspension.

codeSystem.label: Code System
codeSystem.hint: Use ATC for general medication classification. Switch to NHI Drug Code when you have a Taiwan NHI medication code.

code.label: Medication Code
code.hint: Enter the medication code from the selected coding system, for example N02BE01 for ATC.

name.label: Medication Name
name.hint: Enter the medication name that clinicians or patients can recognize.

form.label: Dose Form
form.hint: Choose the real dosage form used in practice, such as tablet, capsule, or injection.

medicationRequest.missingMedication: Please complete Step 8 (Medication) before creating a prescription.
medicationRequest.introHint: A simple way to fill this step is: how much each time, how often, and by which route.
medicationRequest.example1: Acetaminophen 500 mg by mouth three times daily can be entered as: 500 / mg / TID / Oral.
medicationRequest.example2: For nasal sprays or topical medicines, choose the correct route instead of leaving it only in free-text notes.

doseValue.label: Dose
doseValue.hint: Enter the single-dose numeric value, for example 500.

doseUnit.label: Unit
doseUnit.hint: Enter the matching unit, such as mg, mL, or tablet.

frequency.label: Frequency
frequency.hint: If the abbreviations are unfamiliar, use the dropdown as a reference for QD, BID, TID, QID, and PRN.

route.label: Route
route.hint: The selected route is stored as a standard route code. Common examples are Oral and Intravenous (IV).

durationDays.label: Duration (days)
durationDays.hint: For short-term medication, common values are 3, 5, or 7 days. Leave it empty when the duration is not fixed.

note.label: Patient Instructions
note.hint: Write instructions in patient-friendly language, such as take after meals or take before bedtime.

extension.introHint: Supplemental data is useful for information that falls outside the core profile but still needs to be preserved, such as special administration notes or care reminders.
extension.example1: Type Code: special-instruction / Type Name: Special Instruction
extension.example2: Extension URL: use a stable definition URL, for example https://example.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/special-instruction
extension.example3: Extension Value: Take 30 minutes after meals and avoid antacids together.

codeCode.label: Type Code
codeCode.hint: Use a short machine-readable identifier, such as special-instruction.

codeDisplay.label: Type Name
codeDisplay.hint: Use a human-readable title, such as Special administration instruction.

ext1Url.label: Extension URL (1)
ext1Url.hint: Enter the definition URL for this extension. If the same meaning is reused later, keep the same URL.

ext1Value.label: Extension Value (1)
ext1Value.hint: Enter the actual supplemental content, for example Take 30 minutes after meals and avoid antacids together.

ext2Url.label: Extension URL (2)
ext2Url.hint: If you need a second supplemental entry, provide another extension URL here. Leave it blank if not needed.

ext2Value.label: Extension Value (2)
ext2Value.hint: A second supplemental note, reminder, or other free-text extension value.

summary.title: Prescription Summary Review
summary.description: Review the core prescription details before submitting. No need to inspect raw JSON first.
summary.reviewHint: If anything looks incorrect, go back to the earlier steps and update it before submitting.
